project_name: Change-type-driven branch naming and versioning policy
global_objective: Make a change's explicit Change type (recorded in spec-writer) the single source of truth for both plan-executor's branch prefix and the semver segment expected at landing, with a per-project .sdd-kit.json policy that drives non-blocking checks in scripts/validate.sh and an archive-blocking gate in verify.

tasks.justification: Editing an interactive skill's instructions and template following the spec; no code/tests (all four ACs are manual behavioral checks), so bounded doc work fits doc_writer/sonnet.
tasks.instructions: Update the spec-writer skill so it records one explicit Change type per spec, per R1.S1-R1.S4. Edit plugins/sdd-kit/skills/spec-writer/SKILL.md and assets/interview-steps.md to add a change-type step right after the one-liner/purpose is established (R1.S1): present feat/fix/chore/refactor/docs with one recommended from the one-liner. Handle a one-liner mixing a fix and new capability by surfacing the dominant-side/split tradeoff instead of guessing (R1.S2). Handle the leading command argument: when /sdd-kit:spec's first word case-insensitively matches one of the five types, skip the question, treat the rest as the one-liner, echo the type back in one line, and record it (R1.S3); when it does not match, treat all text as the one-liner and ask interactively (R1.S4). Add a `Change type: <value>` line near the top of the written spec in assets/spec-template.md (just under the title/Purpose). Update plugins/sdd-kit/commands/spec.md argument-hint if needed to reflect the optional leading type word. Do not touch git.mjs or any exec/verify script (those are separate tasks). AC1-AC4 are all [manual] so no test is written here.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Edited spec-writer SKILL.md, interview-steps.md, spec-template.md (with a `Change type:` line) and commands/spec.md, so the interview records an explicit Change type and honors a valid leading type argument.

tasks.justification: Bounded Node ESM change to branch creation plus a reusable .sdd-kit.json reader, with clear scenario-derived acceptance criteria.
tasks.instructions: Implement R2.S1-R2.S3. Introduce a reusable .sdd-kit.json config reader (repo-root, all fields optional) exposing branchPrefixes, versioningPolicy (default 'disabled'), and changelogPath (default 'CHANGELOG.md') so tasks t4-versioning-check, t5-validate-wiring and t6-verify-gate can reuse it. Read the spec's `Change type` line (the line format produced by t1-spec-writer-change-type) and map it to a branch prefix through branchPrefixes, falling back per-key to the built-in identity default map (feat/fix/chore/refactor/docs) when the key is missing or the file is absent (R2.S1). Support an explicit empty-string prefix producing a branch named exactly <slug> with no leading slash (R2.S2). When the spec has no `Change type` line, default to feat (today's behavior) and make init's output include a note recommending the spec be updated with an explicit Change type (R2.S3). Modify plugins/sdd-kit/scripts/exec/git.mjs (ensureBranch currently hardcodes `feat/${slug}`) to accept the resolved prefix/branch name, and wire the resolution into cmdInit in plugins/sdd-kit/scripts/exec-tools.mjs. Add unit tests alongside the existing test/exec/git.test.mjs style covering R2.S1/S2/S3. Node ESM stdlib only, no network.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Modified git.mjs ensureBranch (parameterized prefix), exec-tools.mjs cmdInit wiring, a new .sdd-kit.json reader module, and passing unit tests for the three branch-naming scenarios.

test_contract.assertion: A fix-typed spec with no project config produces a branch prefixed fix rather than feat
A project config mapping a type to an empty prefix produces a branch equal to the slug with no leading slash
A spec with no recorded change type still creates a feat-prefixed branch and init reports a note recommending an explicit change type be added

tasks.justification: The deliverable pairs an AGENTS.md documentation edit with a re-runnable check so its [auto] AC (AC8) can be mechanically verified, which the code_writer test contract provides.
tasks.instructions: Implement R3.S1. Edit the repo-root AGENTS.md so its versioning section documents, per change type, which semver segment a landing bumps (fix/chore/refactor to patch, feat to minor, docs to no bump required, major reserved and unused pre-1.0.0), replacing the current vague 'bump the version' guidance. In the Branch naming section, rename the `spec/<slug>` entry to `docs/<slug>`. Add a re-runnable test (grep-based, picked up by the existing test runner) asserting both the segment table content and that `docs/<slug>` is present while `spec/<slug>` is gone, so AC8 is mechanically checkable. Do not modify any script behavior beyond adding this assertion test.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Edited AGENTS.md with an explicit change-type-to-segment table and the docs/<slug> branch rename, plus a grep-based test asserting that content.

test_contract.assertion: AGENTS.md's versioning section states the per-type segment rule (fix/chore/refactor to patch, feat to minor, docs no bump, major reserved pre-1.0.0) and its branch-naming section lists docs/<slug> instead of spec/<slug>

tasks.justification: Non-trivial deterministic module (git-diff scoping, per-plugin bump/changelog detection, segment reverse-mapping) with clear scenario-derived acceptance criteria; the meatiest code task.
tasks.instructions: Implement the policy-driven versioning check for R4.S2-R4.S5 as a reusable Node ESM module. Reuse the .sdd-kit.json reader from t2-branch-prefix to obtain versioningPolicy and changelogPath. Reuse the change-type-to-segment table introduced in AGENTS.md by t3-agents-md-versioning (encode the same mapping in code). For versioningPolicy 'plugin-changelog': for each plugin whose files a branch touched (layout plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json and plugins/<name>/CHANGELOG.md per the spec's Assumptions), detect whether plugin.json's version was bumped and whether a new CHANGELOG.md heading was added, and whether the bumped segment matches the segment expected for the branch's type (reverse-mapped through branchPrefixes). Emit a warning naming the plugin and the specific gap: missing bump and/or missing changelog entry (R4.S3), or wrong segment stating branch type, actual segment and expected segment (R4.S4). A fully compliant plugin yields no warning (R4.S2). For 'changelog-only' (honoring changelogPath, default CHANGELOG.md): warn only when non-trivial code changes lack a new entry, with no segment check (R4.S5). Return warnings as data (do not exit non-zero). Add unit tests with fixtures for each scenario. This module is consumed by t5-validate-wiring and t6-verify-gate.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A Node ESM versioning-check module returning a list of warnings for plugin-changelog (missing bump, missing changelog, wrong segment) and changelog-only (missing entry) policies, with unit tests covering R4.S2-R4.S5.

test_contract.assertion: Under plugin-changelog, a plugin with a correctly-segmented version bump and a matching new changelog heading produces no warning
Under plugin-changelog, a touched plugin missing its version bump and/or a new changelog heading produces a warning naming the plugin and the specific missing piece
Under plugin-changelog, a plugin bumped by the wrong segment produces a warning stating the branch type, the segment bumped, and the segment expected
Under changelog-only, non-trivial changes without a new entry in the configured changelog file produce a warning with no segment-level check

tasks.justification: Bounded integration wiring the check into the existing bash gate without ever altering its exit code; clear acceptance criterion.
tasks.instructions: Implement R4.S1: wire the versioning-check module built by t4-versioning-check into scripts/validate.sh as a non-blocking step that reads versioningPolicy from the repo-root .sdd-kit.json (via the reader from t2-branch-prefix; segment table from t3-agents-md-versioning) and prints any returned warnings but NEVER changes validate.sh's exit code. When versioningPolicy is 'disabled', absent, or unset, print no version/changelog warning at all. Add a test asserting that on a disabled/absent policy no warning is printed and validate.sh still exits 0.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Modified scripts/validate.sh invoking the versioning-check module non-blockingly, plus a test proving disabled/absent policy prints nothing and exit code stays 0.

test_contract.assertion: With versioningPolicy disabled or absent, running scripts/validate.sh prints no version/changelog warning and exits 0

tasks.justification: Bounded change to verify-tools' archive gate reusing an existing module, with five scenario-derived acceptance criteria.
tasks.instructions: Implement R5.S1-R5.S5. Add a policy-driven pre-archive gate to plugins/sdd-kit/scripts/verify-tools.mjs so that, when all ACs are green and archiveIfGreen would otherwise run, it first runs the versioning check from t4-versioning-check scoped to the files the spec's execution touched. Under 'disabled'/unset policy, archive exactly as today with no extra check (R5.S1). Under 'plugin-changelog': archive when every touched plugin has a matching bump and changelog entry (R5.S2); refuse to archive and report exactly which plugin is missing its bump and/or changelog when one is missing (R5.S3); when bump+changelog exist but the segment is wrong, print a warning naming the mismatch but still archive (R5.S4). Under 'changelog-only', refuse to archive and report the missing changelog entry when non-trivial changes lack one (R5.S5). Update plugins/sdd-kit/skills/verify/SKILL.md prose to document this gate. Add unit tests in the existing verify-tools test style covering all five scenarios.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Modified verify-tools.mjs archive gate plus verify SKILL.md prose and unit tests covering R5.S1-R5.S5 (disabled no-op, compliant archives, missing bump/changelog blocks, wrong segment warns-not-blocks, changelog-only missing entry blocks).

test_contract.assertion: With policy disabled or unset, a spec with all ACs green archives with no additional check
With plugin-changelog and every touched plugin compliant, verify archives normally
With plugin-changelog and a touched plugin missing its bump and/or changelog, verify does not archive and reports the specific plugin
With plugin-changelog and a touched plugin whose segment is wrong (bump and changelog present), verify warns about the mismatch but still archives
With changelog-only and a missing required changelog entry, verify does not archive and reports the missing entry

tasks.justification: Small but [auto]-verified deliverable: writes the repo's own config and a test asserting its contents, so it is a code_writer task rather than a pure file drop.
tasks.instructions: Create this repo's own root .sdd-kit.json for AC19: set versioningPolicy to 'plugin-changelog' and a branchPrefixes map consistent with the renamed docs/<slug> convention documented in AGENTS.md by t3-agents-md-versioning (and parseable by the reader from t2-branch-prefix). Without this the feature stays inert here (default policy is disabled). Add a test asserting the file exists with versioningPolicy 'plugin-changelog' and a branchPrefixes map matching AGENTS.md's prefixes (feat/fix/chore/refactor/docs), per AC19 and the compliant-landing expectation in R4.S2.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A root .sdd-kit.json with versioningPolicy plugin-changelog and an AGENTS.md-consistent branchPrefixes map, plus a test asserting its presence and contents.

test_contract.assertion: The repo root .sdd-kit.json exists with versioningPolicy plugin-changelog and a branchPrefixes map consistent with AGENTS.md's docs/<slug> branch convention

tasks.justification: Integrative confirmation of the full fix-classified flow end to end; a manual (AC-E2E [manual]) judgment across all prior tasks' outputs, not a re-runnable unit test.
tasks.instructions: Confirm R-E2E.S1 (satisfying AC-E2E, which is [manual]) by exercising the full flow on a throwaway spec authored with Change type: fix touching plugins/sdd-kit/, combining the outputs of t1-spec-writer-change-type, t2-branch-prefix, t3-agents-md-versioning, t4-versioning-check, t5-validate-wiring, t6-verify-gate and t7-repo-config: plan-executor creates a fix/<slug> branch; before any version bump exists verify refuses to archive and reports the missing bump/changelog for sdd-kit; after a patch-segment bump and a matching CHANGELOG.md entry are added, scripts/validate.sh prints no warning for sdd-kit and re-running verify archives the spec. Produce an end-to-end verification report of each step's result.
tasks.expected_output_schema: An end-to-end verification report walking the fix-classified flow: fix/<slug> branch created, verify blocks before the bump with a specific report, and archives successfully once a correct patch bump + changelog entry exist.
